title: 概述
content: 后台任务让你能够启动长时间运行的操作，然后在完成时检查结果，而不阻塞你的工作流。

title: 前台 vs 后台对比
content: 前台任务（默认）： 你: 运行测试 → [等待测试完成...] → [5分钟后] ✓ 测试完成  后台任务： 你: 运行测试（后台） → [立即返回] Task started: task_001 [你可以继续工作...]  [稍后] 你: 检查任务状态 ✓ task_001 完成！

title: 核心概念
content: 后台任务允许： - 非阻塞执行：任务在后台运行，你可以继续对话 - 自动通知：任务完成后自动通知你 - 结果获取：随时查看任务输出和状态

title: Bash 后台任务
content: 在 Bash 工具中设置 run_in_background: true { "command": "npm test", "run_in_background": true }  特点： - 命令在后台执行 - 会话可以继续其他对话 - 完成后收到 <task-notification> 通知

title: Agent 后台任务
content: 使用 Agent 工具的后台模式 { "subagent_type": "Explore", "prompt": "搜索所有 API 端点", "description": "探索 API 结构", "run_in_background": true }  特点： - 子代理独立运行 - 不阻塞主会话 - 完成后自动通知

title: 查看任务列表
content: 使用 /tasks 命令查看所有后台任务：  /tasks  输出示例： Running Tasks: - a024a90: 探索 API 结构 (running) - a195852d: 运行测试 (completed)  Background Shells: - shell_001: npm build (running)

title: 获取任务输出
content: 使用 TaskOutput 工具：  获取任务结果 { "task_id": "a024a90f936a08281", "block": false  // 不阻塞，只检查状态 }  或阻塞等待完成 { "task_id": "a024a90f936a08281", "block": true, "timeout": 60000  // 等待最多 60 秒 }

title: 停止任务
content: 使用 TaskStop 工具：  { "task_id": "a024a90f936a08281" }

title: 案例：长时间构建
content: 启动构建，继续其他工作 "运行 npm build 在后台，我继续写文档"  Claude 执行： { "command": "npm run build", "run_in_background": true }  你可以继续： "现在帮我写 README"  构建完成后收到通知 <task-notification task_id="...">构建完成</task-notification>

title: 案例：并行探索
content: 同时启动多个探索任务 "并行探索： 1. 搜索认证相关代码 2. 搜索数据库相关代码 3. 搜索 API 相关代码  都完成后汇总。"  Claude 并行启动 3 个后台 Agent

title: 案例：测试执行
content: 后台运行测试套件 "在后台运行完整测试套件，我继续开发"  { "command": "pytest tests/ -v", "run_in_background": true, "timeout": 300000  // 5 分钟超时 }

title: 案例：批量并行操作
content: 安装所有微服务的依赖 "安装所有微服务的依赖"  Claude: 发现 5 个微服务  → cd service-user && npm install & [task_001] → cd service-order && npm install & [task_002] → cd service-payment && npm install & [task_003] → cd service-notif && npm install & [task_004] → cd service-frontend && npm install & [task_005]  ✓ 已并行启动 5 个安装任务  [10分钟后...]  你: 检查安装状态  Claude: task_001: ✅ 完成 task_002: ✅ 完成 task_003: ✅ 完成 task_004: 🔄 85% task_005: ✅ 完成

title: 案例：长时间模型训练
content: 启动模型训练，需要几小时 "启动模型训练，需要几小时"  Claude: → python train.py & [task_ml_001]  ✓ 训练已在后台启动 日志文件: logs/training.log  [训练在后台运行...]  [2小时后...]  你: 训练完成了吗？  Claude: task_ml_001: ✅ 完成 运行时间: 2h 15m 30s  查看结果： → 显示训练日志 → 评估模型性能

title: 什么是 `/loop`？
content: /loop 让 Claude Code 在本地长时间运行一个任务，最多支持 3 天无人值守。  持续监控构建状态，失败时自动修复 /loop 每 10 分钟检查构建状态，失败时自动修复  Claude: → 设置循环任务 → 每 10 分钟自动执行 → 检查构建 → 失败则分析修复 → 重新构建  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 循环任务已启动 任务：检查构建状态 间隔：10 分钟 最长运行：3 天 状态：运行中 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

title: `/loop` 使用示例
content: 持续监控测试 /loop 每 5 分钟运行测试，失败时自动修复  持续监控部署 /loop 每分钟检查部署是否完成  持续检查错误日志 /loop 每 15 分钟检查错误日志，发现新错误时报告  使用 loop skill 控制循环 /loop-status    查看当前循环状态 /loop-start     启动新循环 /loop-stop      停止循环

title: `/loop` 最佳实践
content: 适用场景 - CI/CD 监控与自动修复 - 长时间数据同步监控 - 持续性代码质量检查 - 等待外部服务响应  注意事项 - 设置合理的检查间隔（建议 >= 5 分钟） - 循环任务会占用会话资源 - 重要循环应记录日志便于追溯

title: 什么是 `/schedule`？
content: /schedule 通过 CronCreate 工具在云端设置定时任务，Claude 会在指定时间自动执行。  每天早上 9 点检查依赖更新 /schedule 每天早上 9 点检查依赖更新  Claude: → 创建定时任务 → 每天 09:00 在云端自动执行 → 检查 npm audit → 如果有更新，生成报告

title: `/schedule` 使用示例
content: 每天凌晨运行安全扫描 /schedule 每天 02:00 运行 npm audit 并报告漏洞  每周一早上生成周报 /schedule 每周一 09:00 总结上周的 git commit 统计  工作日每天检查测试覆盖率 /schedule 工作日 18:00 运行测试并检查覆盖率

title: Cron 表达式格式
content: 使用标准 5 字段 cron 表达式（本地时区）：  分钟 小时 日 月 星期  示例： 0 9          每天 09:00 0 2          每天 02:00 0 9   1-5     工作日 09:00 0 9   1       每周一 09:00 /5         每 5 分钟 0           每小时整点

title: `/schedule` 配置选项
content: 持久化定时任务（会话重启后继续） { "cron": "0 9   1-5", "prompt": "检查测试覆盖率", "recurring": true, "durable": true }

title: 日志位置
content: ~/.claude/tasks/ ├── task_001.log ├── task_002.log ├── task_003.log └── scheduled/ └── daily_audit.log

title: 查看日志
content: 查看特定任务的日志 "显示 task_001 的日志"  Claude: → tail -n 50 ~/.claude/tasks/task_001.log  [2024-03-15 14:25:00] Task started [2024-03-15 14:25:01] Running: npm test [2024-03-15 14:30:15] Test complete [2024-03-15 14:30:15] All tests passed

title: 日志最佳实践
content: 重要任务应保存日志 - 长时间训练任务 - 数据迁移任务 - 批量处理任务  日志管理建议 - 定期清理旧日志（超过 7 天） - 重要日志复制到项目目录 - 使用时间戳便于追溯

title: 实时状态监控
content: 查看所有任务状态 /tasks  查看特定任务详情 "查看 task_001 的详细状态"  监控长时间任务 "每 5 分钟汇报 task_ml_001 的进度"

title: 调试技巧
content: 1. 查看任务输出 { "task_id": "...", "block": false  // 快速查看状态 }  2. 检查日志文件 → Read ~/.claude/tasks/task_xxx.log  3. 测试命令单独运行 先前台运行验证命令正确性，再后台化  4. 分阶段执行 复杂任务拆分为多个小任务

title: 状态流转
content: graph LR A[pending] --> B[running] B --> C[completed] B --> D[failed] B --> E[stopped]

title: 状态查询
content: 使用 TaskOutput 查询状态 { "task_id": "...", "block": false  // 只查询，不等待 }  返回状态信息 { "status": "running", "progress": "50%" }

title: ✅ DO - 应该做的
content: 1. 长时间任务使用后台模式 构建大型项目 → 后台 运行完整测试 → 后台 数据库迁移 → 后台 模型训练 → 后台  2. 监控任务状态 定期检查后台任务 收到通知后及时处理结果 保存重要任务日志  3. 使用并行处理 独立任务可以并行运行 批量操作使用并行策略 限制并行数量避免资源耗尽  4. 设置合理的超时 根据任务类型设置 timeout { "command": "npm test", "timeout": 120000  // 2 分钟 }  { "command": "python train.py", "timeout": 3600000  // 1 小时 }  5. 持续监控使用 /loop CI/CD 自动修复 /loop 每 10 分钟检查构建状态，失败时自动修复  部署监控 /loop 每分钟检查部署是否完成

title: ❌ DON'T - 避免做的
content: 1. 启动太多并行任务 错误：同时启动 20 个任务 正确：限制在 5 个以内，分批次执行  2. 忘记检查结果 错误：启动后台任务后忽略通知 正确：收到通知后立即查看输出  3. 不监控失败的任务 错误：任务失败后不处理 正确：查看日志，分析原因，必要时重试  4. 轮询后台任务状态 错误：手动轮询 while running: check status  正确：等待自动通知 启动任务 → 继续其他工作 → 收到通知  5. 在后台运行交互命令 错误：后台运行需要输入的命令 npm init -- 后台运行  正确：前台运行交互命令 npm init

title: 任务完成通知
content: <task-notification> <task-id>a024a90...</task-id> <output-file>/tmp/output.txt</output-file> </task-notification>

title: 处理通知
content: 收到通知后： 1. 使用 Read 工具读取输出文件 2. 或使用 TaskOutput 获取完整结果  读取输出文件 { "file_path": "/tmp/output.txt" }

title: 🎯 练习 1：后台构建
content: 在 Claude Code 中： "在后台运行 npm run build，完成后告诉我结果"  焦点放在这，让我继续其他工作 "同时帮我写一个简单的测试用例"

title: 🎯 练习 2：并行探索
content: "启动 3 个后台 Explore Agent： 1. 探索 src/ 目录结构 2. 搜索所有 async 函数 3. 搜索所有 TODO 注释  完成后汇总报告。"

title: 🎯 练习 3：任务监控
content: "启动一个长时间测试，然后： - 用 /tasks 查看状态 - 继续其他对话 - 收到通知后检查结果"

title: 🎯 练习 4：循环监控
content: 设置一个持续监控任务 /loop 每 5 分钟检查是否有新的错误日志  查看循环状态 /loop-status  停止循环 /loop-stop

title: 🎯 练习 5：定时任务
content: 创建一个定时任务 /schedule 每天 09:00 检查 git 状态  查看定时任务列表 使用 CronList 工具查看已设置的定时任务

title: 🎯 练习 6：批量并行
content: 模拟批量操作 "假设有 3 个子项目，并行安装它们的依赖： - project-a - project-b - project-c  完成后汇总安装结果。"

content: - [多 Agent 协作](../11-multi-agent/) - 并行 Agent 执行策略 - [CLI 命令](../10-cli/) - /tasks 命令详解 - [Subagents 参考](../04-subagents/) - Agent 后台模式 - [Checkpoints](../08-checkpoints/) - 任务状态快照 - [CronCreate 工具文档](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude-code/cron)

content: 掌握后台任务后，继续学习 [Checkpoints](../08-checkpoints/) 了解会话快照与回滚。

title: 概述
content: Channels 是 Claude Code 的消息推送机制，通过 MCP 协议实现实时通信，支持 Discord、Slack 等平台集成。

title: 什么是 Channels
content: Channels 是基于 MCP（Model Context Protocol）的消息通道： - 实时推送：主动发送消息到外部平台 - 双向通信：接收外部指令和反馈 - 事件驱动：响应代码事件自动触发

title: 核心概念
content: graph LR A[Claude Code] --> B[MCP Server] B --> C[Discord] B --> D[Slack] B --> E[Custom Endpoint] C --> F[用户反馈] D --> F F --> A

title: MCP Server 配置
content: 在 ~/.claude/settings.json 中配置：  { "mcpServers": { "discord-channel": { "command": "node", "args": ["mcp-discord-server.js"], "env": { "DISCORD_TOKEN": "your-token", "CHANNEL_ID": "your-channel-id" } }, "slack-channel": { "command": "python", "args": ["mcp_slack_server.py"], "env": { "SLACK_BOT_TOKEN": "xoxb-...", "CHANNEL_ID": "C..." } } } }

title: Channel 工具
content: MCP Channels 提供的工具：

title: 场景 1：构建通知
content: 构建完成后自动通知 Discord "配置 Hook：构建完成后发送 Discord 消息"  Hook 配置 { "hooks": { "Stop": [{ "command": "mcp discord-channel send_message '构建完成'", "description": "通知构建结果" }] } }

title: 场景 2：团队协作
content: 通过 Slack 接收团队反馈 "检查 Slack channel 中的新消息"  Claude 使用 MCP 工具 mcp__slack-channel__receive_messages  处理反馈 "根据团队反馈修改代码"

title: 场景 3：事件订阅
content: 订阅代码变更事件 "当检测到 git commit 时，通知 Discord"  配置自动触发 { "hooks": { "PostToolUse": [{ "matcher": "Bash", "command": "mcp discord-channel send_message '新提交：$COMMIT_MSG'", "description": "通知新提交" }] } }

title: 设置 Discord Bot
content: 1. 创建 Discord Bot 2. 获取 Bot Token 3. 获取 Channel ID 4. 配置 MCP Server  { "mcpServers": { "discord": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@anthropic/mcp-discord"], "env": { "DISCORD_TOKEN": "Bot ...", "CHANNEL_ID": "123456789" } } } }

title: Discord 工具使用
content: 发送消息 mcp__discord__send_message({ "content": "构建成功！✅" })  接收消息 mcp__discord__receive_messages({ "limit": 10 })

title: 设置 Slack App
content: 1. 创建 Slack App 2. 获取 Bot Token (xoxb-...) 3. 获取 Channel ID 4. 配置 MCP Server  { "mcpServers": { "slack": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@anthropic/mcp-slack"], "env": { "SLACK_BOT_TOKEN": "xoxb-...", "CHANNEL_ID": "C..." } } } }

title: Slack 工具使用
content: 发送消息 mcp__slack__send_message({ "text": "测试通过 ✅", "channel": "C..." })  接收消息 mcp__slack__receive_messages({ "channel": "C...", "limit": 20 })

title: 创建自定义 MCP Server
content: mcp_custom_channel.py from mcp.server import Server from mcp.types import Tool  server = Server("custom-channel")  @server.tool() async def send_notification(message: str) -> str: """发送通知到自定义端点""" 发送到你的 API response = await post_to_api(message) return f"已发送: {message}"  @server.tool() async def receive_feedback() -> list: """接收反馈""" 从你的 API 获取 return await get_feedback()

title: 配置自定义 Server
content: { "mcpServers": { "custom": { "command": "python", "args": ["mcp_custom_channel.py"] } } }

title: 避免的模式
content: 不要做 - 不要在敏感信息中使用公开 Channel - 不要频繁发送消息（会打扰） - 不要忽略消息格式规范 - 不要阻塞等待 Channel 响应

title: 推荐模式
content: 好习惯 - 使用 Hook 自动触发通知 - 设置合理的消息频率 - 包含上下文信息 - 使用格式化消息（Markdown）

title: Discord 消息格式
content: 使用 Discord Markdown 构建状态: ✅ 成功 分支: main 提交: abc123  变更摘要: - feat: 添加认证 - fix: 修复 bug

title: Slack 消息格式
content: { "text": "构建完成", "blocks": [ { "type": "section", "text": { "type": "mrkdwn", "text": "构建状态: ✅ 成功" } } ] }

title: 🎯 练习 1：配置 Discord
content: 1. 创建 Discord Bot 2. 获取 Token 和 Channel ID 3. 配置 ~/.claude/settings.json 4. 测试发送消息  "测试 Discord Channel：发送 'Hello from Claude Code'"

title: 🎯 练习 2：自动通知
content: 配置 Stop Hook "配置 Hook：每次会话结束时通知 Discord"  编辑 ~/.claude/settings.json { "hooks": { "Stop": [{ "command": "claude mcp discord send_message '会话结束'", "description": "通知会话结束" }] } }

title: 🎯 练习 3：接收反馈
content: "检查 Slack Channel 的最新消息并处理反馈"  Claude 会： 1. 调用 mcp__slack__receive_messages 2. 分析消息内容 3. 根据反馈执行操作

title: 相关资源
content: - [MCP 配置](../05-mcp/) - [Hooks 自动化](../06-hooks/) - [官方 MCP 文档](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) - [MCP Discord Server](https://github.com/anthropics/mcp-discord)

title: Powerup 与 Buddy
description: 1. [概述](概述) 2. [Powerup 交互式课程](powerup-交互式课程) 3. [Buddy 虚拟伙伴](buddy-虚拟伙伴) 4. [最佳实践](最佳实践) 5. [常见问题](常见问题)  ---
keywords:
### 学习路径

**推荐的学习顺序：**

title: 目录
content: 1. [概述](概述) 2. [Powerup 交互式课程](powerup-交互式课程) 3. [Buddy 虚拟伙伴](buddy-虚拟伙伴) 4. [最佳实践](最佳实践) 5. [常见问题](常见问题)  ---

title: 什么是 Powerup？
content: Powerup 是 Claude Code 的交互式学习系统，通过动画演示和互动练习教你掌握 Claude Code 的各种功能。  核心特点： - 📚 交互式课程 - 分步学习各种功能 - 🎬 动画演示 - 可视化展示功能使用方法 - 🎯 实战练习 - 在实际环境中练习技能 - 📊 进度追踪 - 记录学习进度和成就

title: 什么是 Buddy？
content: Buddy 是 Claude Code 的虚拟伙伴系统，你可以孵化并陪伴一个小生物一起写代码。  核心特点： - 🥚 孵化系统 - 孵化属于你的独特伙伴 - 👀 陪伴编程 - 小伙伴看着你写代码 - 🎮 互动体验 - 与伙伴进行简单互动 - 🌟 收集要素 - 解锁不同种类的伙伴  ---

title: 启动 Powerup
content: 在 Claude Code 中输入：  /powerup  系统会显示可用的课程列表，你可以选择想要学习的内容。

title: 可用课程
content: Powerup 提供多种课程，涵盖 Claude Code 的核心功能：

title: 课程结构
content: 每个 Powerup 课程遵循统一的结构：  1. 概念介绍 ├── 什么是这个功能？ ├── 为什么需要它？ └── 什么时候使用？  2. 动画演示 ├── 可视化展示功能 ├── 步骤标注 └── 关键操作高亮  3. 互动练习 ├── 跟随练习 ├── 实时反馈 └── 错误纠正  4. 总结与挑战 ├── 知识点回顾 ├── 进阶挑战 └── 相关课程推荐

title: 学习路径
content: 推荐的学习顺序：  graph TD A[入门基础] --> B[Slash Commands] B --> C[Memory 系统] C --> D[Skills 创建] D --> E[Hooks 自动化] E --> F[MCP 集成] F --> G[多 Agent 协作] G --> H[高级功能]

title: 使用技巧
content: 技巧 1：暂停和继续 - 按 Space 暂停演示 - 按 Enter 继续播放 - 按 Esc 退出课程  技巧 2：重复观看 - 可以随时重新观看课程 - 练习模式可以反复尝试  技巧 3：笔记记录 - Powerup 会记录你的进度 - 查看已完成课程：/powerup --progress  ---

title: 孵化 Buddy
content: 在 Claude Code 中输入：  /buddy  系统会给你一个蛋，你需要通过使用 Claude Code 来孵化它。

title: 孵化过程
content: graph LR A[🥚 蛋] -->|使用 Claude Code| B[🐣 孵化中] B -->|积累互动| C[🐥 小伙伴] C -->|持续使用| D[🌟 成长]

title: 互动方式
content: 与 Buddy 互动的方法：  1. 日常编程 - 每次使用 Claude Code 都会增加亲密度 2. 完成任务 - 完成复杂的编程任务获得额外奖励 3. 特殊事件 - 节日期间有特殊的 Buddy 活动

title: Buddy 种类
content: 根据孵化方式和时间，你可能获得不同种类的 Buddy：

title: Buddy 功能
content: 当前支持的功能： - 在终端角落显示 Buddy - Buddy 会根据你的操作做出反应 - 长时间不使用会进入休眠状态

title: 注意事项
content: > ⚠️ 重要提示： > - Buddy 功能主要是趣味性质，不影响 Claude Code 的核心功能 > - Buddy 数据存储在本地，清除缓存会重置 > - 此功能可能在未来版本中有所变化  ---

title: Powerup 学习建议
content: 1. 循序渐进 - 按推荐顺序学习，打好基础 2. 动手实践 - 学习后立即在实际项目中应用 3. 定期复习 - 隔一段时间重温课程，巩固记忆 4. 记录笔记 - 将重要知识点记录下来

title: Buddy 使用建议
content: 1. 自然使用 - 不要刻意刷亲密度，正常使用即可 2. 享受过程 - Buddy 是编程旅程的陪伴，不必太在意收集 3. 分享乐趣 - 与社区分享你的 Buddy 截图  ---

title: Powerup 相关
content: Q: Powerup 课程会更新吗？ A: 会的。Claude Code 每次大版本更新通常会增加新的课程。  Q: 可以跳过课程吗？ A: 可以，但建议按顺序学习以获得完整体验。  Q: 进度会同步吗？ A: Powerup 进度存储在本地，不会跨设备同步。

title: Buddy 相关
content: Q: Buddy 会消失吗？ A: Buddy 会一直陪伴你，除非你清除 Claude Code 的本地数据。  Q: 可以同时拥有多个 Buddy 吗？ A: 目前每个会话只能有一个活跃的 Buddy。  Q: Buddy 会影响性能吗？ A: 不会，Buddy 只是简单的视觉元素，对性能影响微乎其微。  ---

title: 相关资源
content: - [Slash Commands 参考](../01-slash-commands/) - [Skills 创建指南](../03-skills/) - [官方文档](https://code.claude.com/docs)  ---

title: 🎯 练习 1：完成一个 Powerup 课程
content: 1. 打开 Claude Code 2. 输入 /powerup 3. 选择"入门基础"课程 4. 完成所有章节

title: 🎯 练习 2：孵化你的 Buddy
content: 1. 输入 /buddy 2. 使用 Claude Code 编写一些代码 3. 观察 Buddy 的孵化进度 4. 与你的新伙伴互动  ---  > 💡 提示： Powerup 和 Buddy 是 Claude Code 团队在功能创新上的尝试。它们让学习编程变得更加有趣。期待更多这样的功能在未来版本中出现！

title: Overview
content: Enterprise deployment of Claude Code requires careful consideration of security, compliance, team collaboration, and operational efficiency. This module covers the essential patterns for production-ready deployments.

title: Core Workflows
content: Mastering core workflows dramatically improves development efficiency in enterprise environments.

title: Code Review Workflow
content: Standard Review Process  User: Review this PR's code changes  Claude: → Read changed files → Analyze code quality → Check potential issues → Provide improvement suggestions  Review Report ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  Strengths: - Clean code structure - Comprehensive error handling  Needs Improvement: - src/auth.js:45 - Unhandled Promise rejection - src/user.ts:120 - Missing input validation  Suggestions: - Add try-catch wrapper - Use Zod for schema validation  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

title: Code Review Checklist
content: Run these checks: 1. Run linter (npm run lint) 2. Run type check (npm run type-check) 3. Run tests (npm test) 4. Check security issues 5. Generate coverage report  Output format: - CRITICAL: Blocking issues (must fix) - WARNING: Improvement suggestions (should fix) - INFO: Good practices (acknowledge)

title: Debugging Workflow
content: Systematic Debugging  User: Help debug this bug: user login failure  Claude: [Phase 1] Information Gathering → View error logs → Check related code → Understand reproduction steps  [Phase 2] Problem Identification → Analyze error stack → Check data flow → Verify hypothesis  [Phase 3] Fix & Verification → Implement fix → Add tests → Verify fix  Bug Fixed Cause: Missing secret key during JWT token verification Location: src/auth.js:78 Fix: Added environment variable check  Debugging Best Practice

title: Refactoring Workflow
content: Safe Refactoring Process  User: Refactor this component to improve performance  Claude: [Step 1] Understand Code → Analyze component structure → Identify performance bottlenecks → Assess impact scope  [Step 2] Plan → Use plan mode → List refactoring steps → Assess risks  [Step 3] Execute → Small iterations → Keep tests passing → Gradual optimization  [Step 4] Verify → Run all tests → Performance comparison → Code review  Refactoring Complete Performance improvement: 60% Tests: All passing  Refa

title: Pre-Refactoring Checklist
content: - [ ] Backup current code - [ ] Run test suite - [ ] Record current performance metrics - [ ] Identify dependencies

title: During Refactoring
content: - [ ] Small commits - [ ] Run tests after each step - [ ] Keep functionality unchanged - [ ] Update documentation

title: Post-Refactoring
content: - [ ] All tests pass - [ ] Performance improvement verified - [ ] Code review completed - [ ] API documentation updated

title: Remote Control & Sessions
content: Claude Code supports remote session management for seamless multi-device workflows.

title: Remote Control Basics
content: Remote control lets you continue a locally running Claude Code session from your phone, tablet, or any browser.  Local Machine                      Cloud/Remote Machine |                                    | |  Claude Code CLI                   | |  (Terminal)                        | |         |                          | |         | SSH/Remote Protocol      | |         v                          | |  +------------------+              | |  |  Remote Session  |              | |  |  Manager

title: Starting Remote Control
content: From CLI:  Start with default session name claude remote-control  Start with custom name claude remote-control --name "Auth Refactor"  From within session:  /remote-control /remote-control "Auth Refactor"  Available Flags:

title: Connecting to Sessions
content: Three ways to connect from other devices:  1. Session URL - Printed to terminal at startup; open in any browser 2. QR Code - Press spacebar after startup to display scannable QR code 3. Find by Name - Browse your sessions at claude.ai/code or in Claude mobile app (iOS/Android)

title: SSH Remote Sessions
content: Basic SSH Remote Usage  SSH connection and use Claude Code ssh user@remote-server  On remote server cd /path/to/project claude  Claude Code now runs on remote server All operations execute remotely  SSH Config Optimization  ~/.ssh/config  Host production HostName prod.example.com User deploy IdentityFile ~/.ssh/prod_key ServerAliveInterval 60 ServerAliveCountMax 3  Host staging HostName staging.example.com User deploy IdentityFile ~/.ssh/staging_key

title: Session Management
content: Session Types  +-------------------------------------+ +-------------------------------------+ +-------------------------------------+  Resume Sessions  Continue most recent conversation claude -c  Resume named session claude -r "auth-refactor" "finish this PR"  Fork session for experimentation claude --resume auth-refactor --fork-session "try OAuth instead"

title: Session Checkpoints
content: Creating Checkpoints  User: Create a checkpoint at this critical point  Claude: → Save current state → Record file snapshot → Save context  Checkpoint Created Name: checkpoint-api-complete Time: 2026-04-06 14:30:45 Contains: - 12 modified files - Complete conversation history - Task progress records  ---

title: Structured Logging

title: Prometheus Alert Rules
content: alerts.yml groups: - name: claude_code_alerts interval: 30s rules: High error rate alert - alert: HighErrorRate expr: | rate(claude_commands_total{status="error"}[5m]) / rate(claude_commands_total[5m]) > 0.05 for: 5m labels: severity: warning annotations: summary: "Claude Code error rate is high" description: "Error rate is {{ $value | humanizePercentage }}"  Response time alert - alert: SlowResponseTime expr: | histogram_quantile(0.95, claude_commands_duration_bucket) > 5000 for: 5m labels: se

title: CI/CD Integration
content: Integrate Claude Code into CI/CD pipelines for automated code review, testing, and deployment.

title: GitHub Actions Integration
content: Basic CI Pipeline  .github/workflows/claude-ci.yml name: Claude Code CI  on: push: branches: [main, develop] pull_request: branches: [main, develop]  jobs: claude-check: runs-on: ubuntu-latest  steps: - name: Checkout code uses: actions/checkout@v4 with: fetch-depth: 0   Full history for better analysis  - name: Setup Node.js uses: actions/setup-node@v4 with: node-version: '20'  - name: Install Claude Code run: | npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code claude --version  - name: Install depende

title: GitLab CI Integration
content: .gitlab-ci.yml stages: - check - review - test - deploy  variables: CLAUDE_VERSION: "latest"  Claude Code code check claude:check: stage: check image: node:20 before_script: - npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code - claude --version script: - npm ci - claude -p "Run code quality checks and output JSON report" > claude-report.json artifacts: reports: codequality: claude-report.json paths: - claude-report.json expire_in: 1 week  Claude Code review claude:review: stage: review image: node:20 be

content: Set up OpenTelemetry monitoring:  Step 1: Install Dependencies  npm install @opentelemetry/api @opentelemetry/sdk-node  Step 2: Create Telemetry Module  // telemetry.js const { MeterProvider } = require('@opentelemetry/sdk-metrics');  const meterProvider = new MeterProvider({ exporter: new OTLPGrpcMetricExporter({ url: 'http://localhost:4317', }), interval: 10000, });  const meter = meterProvider.getMeter('claude-code'); module.exports = { meter };  ---

content: // config/environments.json { "development": { "permissions": { "defaultMode": "auto" }, "features": { "enableDebugLogging": true } }, "staging": { "permissions": { "defaultMode": "default" }, "auditLogging": { "enabled": true } }, "production": { "permissions": { "defaultMode": "default" }, "security": { "strictMode": true }, "auditLogging": { "enabled": true, "logLevel": "detailed" } } }

title: Pattern 2: Team Shared Skills
content: Shared skills directory structure /opt/team-claude-skills/ ├── code-review/ │   ├── skill.json │   └── instructions.md ├── deploy-staging/ ├── test-coverage/ └── security-scan/  Configure in CLAUDE.md export CLAUDE_TEAM_SKILLS_PATH=/opt/team-claude-skills

title: Pattern 3: Automated Pre-commit Hooks
content: !/bin/bash .claude/hooks/pre-commit  echo "Running team pre-commit checks..."  1. Check for sensitive information if git diff --cached | grep -iE "(password|api_key|secret)"; then echo "CRITICAL: Potential sensitive information detected!" exit 1 fi  2. Run linter npm run lint -- --quiet if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then echo "Lint failed, please fix before committing" exit 1 fi  3. Run type check npm run type-check -- --quiet if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then echo "Type check failed" exit 1 fi  4. Run fast tests npm t

title: Pattern 4: Deployment Workflow
content: [Phase 1] Pilot → Select 5% users → Deploy new configuration → Monitor for 1 week | v [Phase 2] Gradual Rollout → Expand to 25% users → Collect feedback → Fix issues | v [Phase 3] Full Deployment → Roll out to all users → Continuous monitoring  ---

title: Enterprise Deployment
content: - Use version control for all configuration files - Implement gradual rollout strategies - Enable audit logging for compliance - Conduct regular security reviews - Document all custom configurations

title: Remote Control
content: - Use SSH key authentication for remote connections - Create checkpoints at critical points - Monitor session activity regularly - Protect session sharing links

title: CI/CD Integration
content: - Cache dependencies in CI pipelines - Run independent jobs in parallel - Use matrix strategies for multi-version testing - Protect sensitive information with secrets - Set appropriate timeouts

title: Monitoring
content: - Sample tracing appropriately (10-20%) - Use appropriate log levels - Protect sensitive information in logs - Regularly review key metrics - Set up alerting for critical thresholds  ---

title: Computer Use
content: Computer Use lets Claude Code "see" your screen and control the mouse and keyboard, acting like a remote desktop assistant.

title: Core Concept
content: Traditional Claude Code: You: "Test the login form" Claude: "I can't see the browser. Please screenshot..."  Computer Use: You: "Test the login form" Claude: [Sees screen] → Move cursor to username field → Type test credentials → Click login button → Verify redirect to dashboard → Report test results

title: When to Use Computer Use
content: Ideal scenarios:

title: Example: Web Application Testing
content: You: Visit localhost:3000 and test the registration flow: 1. Create a new user account 2. Log in with those credentials 3. Verify the dashboard loads correctly  Claude: [Step 1] Registration → Navigate to /register → Fill username: test_user_001 → Fill email: test@example.com → Fill password: Test@12345 → Click "Register" ✓ Success - redirected to login  [Step 2] Login → Enter credentials → Click "Login" ✓ Success - redirected to dashboard  [Step 3] Dashboard verification → Check sidebar visibl

title: Example: Responsive Layout Testing
content: You: Check the dashboard at different screen sizes  Claude: [Desktop 1920x1080] → Resize browser → Analyze layout ✓ Sidebar normal ⚠ Charts truncated on right edge  [Tablet 768x1024] → Resize browser ✓ Sidebar collapses to icons ✓ Content responsive  [Mobile 375x812] → Resize browser ⚠ Modal exceeds viewport ❌ Some buttons unclickable  Recommendations: 1. Add overflow-auto to chart containers 2. Full-screen modals on mobile 3. Increase button touch targets

title: Voice Mode
content: Voice Mode enables press-to-talk voice input, letting you speak instead of type.

title: Core Concept
content: Keyboard input: You (typing): "Refactor this component" → Think phrasing → Type characters → Wait for response  Voice input: You (speaking): "Refactor this component" → Speak directly → Instant transmission → Natural conversation flow

title: Interaction Evolution
content: Punch cards → Keyboard → Mouse → Touch → Voice  Each evolution lowered barriers: - Punch cards: Engineers only - Keyboard: General users - Mouse: No command memorization - Touch: Children can use - Voice: Most natural communication

title: Activation
content: Press and hold Spacebar to speak, release to send.  Flow: Hold Space → Speak → Release → Claude processes → Response

title: Supported Languages
content: Voice Mode supports ~20 languages:

title: When to Use Voice vs Keyboard
Hybrid Strategy
content: Golden Rule: Voice for direction, keyboard for details  Workflow example: 1. 🎤 "Refactor the auth module" (intent) 2. ⌨️ Review Claude's plan 3. 🎤 "Step 3 - use OAuth instead" (feedback) 4. ⌨️ Provide config values 5. 🎤 "Looks good, execute" (confirmation)

title: Voice + Computer Use Combination
content: You (voice): "Open the browser, search for Claude Code tutorials, find the GitHub repo link, copy to clipboard"  Claude [Computer Use]: → Open browser → Navigate to search engine → Enter query → Find repo link → Copy to clipboard  Claude: Done! GitHub link copied to clipboard.  Voice Mode + Computer Use = Voice-controlled screen operations  Ideal when hands are occupied with debugging, physical tasks, or ergonomics.

title: Multi-Agent Collaboration
content: Multi-Agent collaboration is Claude Code's most powerful capability, enabling parallel task processing for dramatically faster workflows.

title: From Serial to Parallel
content: Single Agent (serial): You → Claude → Task A → Task B → Task C Time: Linear growth  Multi-Agent (parallel): You → Claude → Agent 1 (Task A) → Agent 2 (Task B) → Agent 3 (Task C) Time: Parallel execution

title: Infrastructure: Git Worktrees
content: Git Worktrees enable multiple working directories from the same repository, each on a different branch.  Traditional (single directory): my-project/ ← Only main branch → Switching requires stash or commit  Worktree approach: my-project/           ← main branch my-project-feature-a/ ← feature-a branch my-project-feature-b/ ← feature-b branch → Work on different branches simultaneously  Starting in a Worktree:  Claude Code auto-manages worktrees claude --worktree  Claude creates: 1. .claude/workt

title: Tmux Integration
content: Tmux enables multiple terminal panes in one window.  Tmux layout: ┌─────────────┬─────────────┐ │  Agent 1    │  Agent 2    │ │  (Pane 1)   │  (Pane 2)   │ ├─────────────┼─────────────┤ │  Agent 3    │  Agent 4    │ │  (Pane 3)   │  (Pane 4)   │ └─────────────┴─────────────┘  Ctrl+B + arrows to switch panes  Workflow:  Create session tmux new -s work  Different panes Pane 1: cd worktree-1 && claude Pane 2: cd worktree-2 && claude Pane 3: cd worktree-3 && claude  Assign tasks per pane Pane 1: "R

title: Agent Teams
content: Agent Teams enable multiple Claude Code sessions to communicate and coordinate.  Single SubAgent: Main Agent → SubAgent → Result One-to-one relationship  Agent Teams: Writer ←→ Reviewer ←→ Tester Many-to-many communication  Writer/Reviewer Pattern:  Writer Agent: → Write feature code → Send to Reviewer  Reviewer Agent: → Review code quality → Suggest improvements → Return to Writer  Writer Agent: → Apply suggestions → Resend to Reviewer  Reviewer Agent: → Approve → Send to Tester  Tester Agent:

title: Coordinator Mode: Four Phases
content: graph LR R[Research] --> S[Synthesis] S --> I[Implementation] I --> V[Verification]

title: `/batch` Batch Processing
content: Process multiple tasks sequentially:  You: /batch Claude: Provide task list  You: 1. Fix 101 login timeout 2. Fix 102 search sort bug 3. Implement 103 CSV export 4. Fix 104 mobile layout  Claude: → Task 1/4: Fix login timeout... ✓ → Task 2/4: Fix sort bug... ✓ → Task 3/4: Implement CSV... ✓ → Task 4/4: Fix layout... ✓  ✅ All complete!  Best practices: - Use for independent tasks - Clear, self-contained descriptions - 5-10 tasks per batch optimal - Save output as logs

title: `/loop` Long-Running Tasks
content: Run tasks repeatedly for up to 3 days:  You: /loop every 10 minutes check build status, auto-fix if failed  Claude: → Setup loop task → Every 10 min: check → analyze → fix → rebuild  Loop task active: Task: Build monitoring Interval: 10 min Max duration: 3 days Status: Running  Examples:  /loop every 5 min run tests, auto-fix failures /loop every hour run performance benchmarks /loop every min check deployment completion

title: `/schedule` Cloud Scheduled Tasks
content: Tasks run on Anthropic infrastructure without local Claude Code:  You: /schedule daily at 9am check dependency updates  Claude: → Create scheduled task → Runs at 09:00 daily → Check npm audit → Report if updates available  Task: Dependency check Frequency: Daily 09:00 Status: Enabled

title: Remote Control
content: Control Claude Code from another device:  Scenario: Work computer: Claude Code running Home laptop: Connect remotely  Work computer: → Claude Code active → Start Remote Control  Home laptop: → Connect to work session → Check progress → Send new commands → Or observe only

title: Mindset Shift: Asynchronous Work
content: Traditional (synchronous): You do A → You do B → You do C Step by step  Agent collaboration (asynchronous): You start Agent A (task A) You start Agent B (task B) You start Agent C (task C) You do other work (even leave) Agents notify when complete  New workflow:  Morning routine: 1. Assign tasks to multiple agents 2. Let them work in parallel 3. You focus on architecture, requirements 4. Afternoon: review results 5. Merge, review, deploy  You're not "using a tool" - you're "managing a team"   -

title: Advanced Commands
content: Master context management with these commands.

title: `/btw` Side-channel Dialogue
content: Ask tangential questions without polluting main context:  Main dialogue: You: "Refactor auth module" Claude: [Working...]  You: /btw What's the difference between JWT and Session? Claude: "JWT is stateless..." [Brief answer, returns to main]  → Main context unaffected → Auth refactor uninterrupted  When to use /btw:

title: `/compact` Context Compression
content: Summarize and compress context when approaching limits:  You: /compact  Claude: → Analyze conversation → Extract key information → Compress context → Free space  Context compressed. Retained: - Task: Refactor auth module - Completed: JWT implementation - Pending: Session management, token refresh  When to /compact:  ✅ Use when: - Conversation very long, near limit - Major task complete, starting new - Claude "forgetting" earlier content  ❌ Don't use when: - Conversation still short - Task needs

title: `/rewind` Rollback
content: Return to previous conversation state, undoing subsequent actions:  Conversation: [State 1] You: "Create user component" [State 2] Claude: [Creates component] [State 3] You: "Add form validation" [State 4] Claude: [Adds validation] [State 5] You: "Actually make it a list" ← Wrong direction [State 6] Claude: [Modified to list] ← Mistake made  You: /rewind 5 → Return to state 5 → State 6 changes undone → You can restate requirement  See [08-checkpoints](../08-checkpoints/) for detailed rollback d

title: Slash Commands: Project-level Customization
content: Store custom commands in .claude/commands/:  Project structure: .claude/ └── commands/ ├── review.md         /project:review ├── deploy.md         /project:deploy └── test-fix.md       /project:test-fix  Example command:  <!-- .claude/commands/review.md --> Review all branch changes, focusing on: 1. Security vulnerabilities 2. Performance issues 3. Code style consistency Output structured review report.  Usage:  You: /project:review  Claude: → Reads review.md instructions → Executes code review

title: Slash Commands vs Skills
content: Selection guide:  Use Slash Commands for: → Simple, one-off instructions → No parameters needed → Quick creation and use  Use Skills for: → Multi-step workflows → Parameterization needed → Conditional logic, error handling → Reuse and sharing

title: Inline Bash Pre-computation
content: Embed dynamic data in commands:  <!-- .claude/commands/pr-status.md --> Current PR status: gh pr view --json title,state,additions,deletions  Analyze PR progress and suggest next steps.  You: /project:pr-status  Claude: → Execute Bash for PR data → Analyze results → Output suggestions  PR 123 status: - Title: "Add user auth" - State: Open - +256/-42 lines - Review: Approved  Suggestion: Ready to merge...

title: Non-interactive Mode (`-p`)
content: Run Claude Code without interactive input:  Pipe input echo "Explain this code" | claude -p  From file claude -p "Review file" < src/utils.ts  Output to file claude -p "Generate README" > README.md  Pipeline processing cat src//.ts | claude -p "Find all TODOs"

title: `--allowedTools` Restriction
content: Limit available tools for safety:  Read only claude -p "Analyze structure" --allowedTools "Read,Glob,Grep"  Specific commands claude -p "Run tests" --allowedTools "Bash(npm test)"  CI/CD safe usage claude -p "Check standards" \ --allowedTools "Read,Glob,Grep,Bash(npm run lint)"  CI/CD integration:  GitHub Actions - name: AI Review run: | claude -p "Review PR changes" \ --allowedTools "Read,Glob,Grep" \ < <(gh pr diff ${{ github.event.number }})  ---

title: Anti-Patterns
content: Avoid these common mistakes to maximize Claude Code effectiveness.

title: Anti-Pattern 1: One Session for Everything
content: ❌ Wrong approach: Same session: "Fix bug" → "Write tests" → "Deploy" → "Design database" → "Write docs" → "..."  Problems: - Context polluted with unrelated info - Claude confuses different task contexts - Later task quality degrades  ✅ Correct approach: One session per task Fix bug → New session → Write tests → New session → Deploy

title: Anti-Pattern 2: Repeated Corrections Go Further Off
content: ❌ Wrong approach: You: "Make button red" Claude: [Changes] You: "Not right, darker" Claude: [Changes] You: "Still wrong, dark red" Claude: [Changes] You: "Forget it, blue instead" → 4 changes, each modifying previous → Code may become unrecognizable  ✅ Correct approach: You: "Make button 1a56db blue" → Precise requirement once  Or: You: /rewind 3 ← Return to initial state You: "Use brand-primary CSS variable"

title: Anti-Pattern 3: Accept Without Verification
content: ❌ Wrong approach: Claude: "Modified auth.js, added password validation" You: [Glanced, looks right] "OK, seems fine" → Edge cases unhandled → Security vulnerabilities missed  ✅ Correct approach: 1. Request explanation "Explain what you changed and why" 2. Test actively "Run tests to check failures" 3. Check edge cases "Empty input, extra long input, special chars?"

title: Anti-Pattern 4: Over-micromanagement
content: ❌ Wrong approach: You: "Create UserController class" Claude: [Creates] You: "Add getUser method" Claude: [Adds] You: "Add id parameter" Claude: [Modifies] You: "Return type UserDTO" Claude: [Modifies] You: "Add @GetMapping annotation" Claude: [Modifies]  → Puppeteering Claude → Wasted interaction rounds → Claude's capabilities unused  ✅ Correct approach: You: "Create UserController with CRUD endpoints: GET /api/users/:id → UserDTO POST /api/users → create PUT /api/users/:id → update DELETE /api

title: Anti-Pattern 5: Vague Requirements
content: ❌ Wrong approach: You: "Optimize performance" You: "Change styling" You: "Make it better"  → Claude guesses intent → Results may not match expectations → Repeated fixes waste time  ✅ Correct approach: You: "Optimize user list load performance, target 3s down to <1s. Main bottleneck: excessive API requests. Consider request merging or caching."  → Clear problem description → Specific target → Suggested direction

title: Anti-Pattern 6: No CLAUDE.md
content: ❌ Without CLAUDE.md: Session 1: "This is a React project..." Session 2: "This is a React project..." Session 3: "This is a React project..." → Repetitive explanations each session → Inconsistent project understanding → Team members work independently  ✅ With CLAUDE.md: Session 1: Claude reads CLAUDE.md → Instant understanding Session 2: Claude reads CLAUDE.md → Instant understanding Session 3: Claude reads CLAUDE.md → Instant understanding → Zero repetition → Consistent understanding → Unified

title: Pattern: Voice + Computer Use for Hands-Free Testing
content: Scenario: Testing while debugging breakpoints  You (voice): "Test the form submission flow" Claude [Computer Use]: → Fill form fields → Click submit → Check for validation errors → Report results  → Hands stay on debugging tools → Voice directs screen operations

title: Pattern: Parallel Feature Development
content: Setup worktrees claude --worktree name=feature-a claude --worktree name=feature-b claude --worktree name=feature-c  Tmux layout tmux new -s dev Split into 3 panes  Each pane runs Claude Pane 1: cd .claude/worktrees/feature-a && claude Pane 2: cd .claude/worktrees/feature-b && claude Pane 3: cd .claude/worktrees/feature-c && claude  Assign tasks Pane 1: "Implement recommendation algorithm" Pane 2: "Create API endpoints" Pane 3: "Build frontend components"  Later: review and merge

title: Pattern: Continuous Integration Monitoring
content: .claude/commands/ci-monitor.md gh run list --limit 5 --json status,conclusion,name Analyze CI status and suggest actions if failures detected.  Run periodically /loop every 5 min /project:ci-monitor

title: Pattern: Writer-Reviewer-Tester Loop
content: Writer Agent: Implement feature ↓ Reviewer Agent: Check code quality ↓ (if issues) Writer Agent: Apply fixes ↓ (loop until approved) Tester Agent: Run test suite ↓ (if failed) Writer Agent: Fix tests ↓ (loop until pass) Merge

title: Recipe: Large Feature Development
content: Goal: Add product recommendations to e-commerce  Step 1: Create worktrees claude --worktree name=recommendation-engine claude --worktree name=api-endpoints claude --worktree name=frontend-ui  Step 2: Parallel agents Agent 1: "Implement collaborative filtering algorithm" Agent 2: "Create /api/recommendations endpoints" Agent 3: "Build recommendation card components"  Step 3: Continuous testing /loop every 15 min run all tests  Step 4: Wait for completion [Do other work...]  Step 5: Review and me

title: Exercise 1: Computer Use Testing
content: Test a web form without manual clicking:  1. Start your dev server: npm run dev 2. In Claude Code: "Use Computer Use to test the registration form at localhost:3000/register" 3. Observe Claude navigating the form 4. Try responsive testing: "Check the form at 768px and 375px widths"

title: Exercise 2: Voice Mode Dialogue
content: Practice voice + keyboard hybrid:  1. Enable Voice Mode: /voice 2. Voice: "Explain the difference between REST and GraphQL" 3. Keyboard: Review Claude's answer 4. Voice: "Show me a GraphQL query example" 5. Keyboard: Copy the query into your project

title: Exercise 3: Multi-Agent Worktree
content: Run two parallel tasks:  Terminal 1 claude --worktree name=refactor-auth In worktree session: "Refactor auth module"  Terminal 2 claude --worktree name=add-payment In worktree session: "Add payment feature"  Both work in parallel Check progress periodically Merge when complete

title: Exercise 4: Advanced Commands
content: Practice context management:  1. Start a long debugging session 2. When context fills: /compact focus: debugging 3. Ask a tangential question: /btw what's RxJS? 4. Realize wrong direction: /rewind 3 5. Start fresh for new task: /clear

title: Exercise 5: Avoid Anti-patterns
content: Identify and fix anti-patterns:  Review your recent Claude Code sessions: 1. Did you mix unrelated tasks? → Separate sessions 2. Did you correct repeatedly? → Specify precisely 3. Did you accept without verify? → Test actively 4. Did you micromanage? → Give complete requirements 5. Did you have vague requests? → Be specific 6. Did you lack CLAUDE.md? → Create project memory  ---

title: Efficient Dialogue Golden Rules
content: 1. One session, one goal → Focus = Quality  2. State requirements once, clearly → Precision = Fewer changes  3. Give direction, not steps → Trust = Efficiency  4. Verify every result → Review = Safety  5. Use side channels and compression → Tools = Smooth flow

title: Computer Use Best Practices
content: ✅ DO: - Treat Claude as patient but slow tester - Give clear, specific operation instructions - Split complex tasks into steps - Provide fallback options (keyboard shortcuts) - Use for exploratory UI testing  ❌ DON'T: - Expect pixel-precision operations - Use for production-critical operations - Apply in time-sensitive automation - Navigate deep nested menus

title: Voice Mode Best Practices
content: ✅ DO: - Describe intent, not details - Use short phrases - Combine voice and keyboard - Confirm important operations  ❌ DON'T: - Discuss sensitive info in public - Voice-input complex code - Speak too fast

title: Multi-Agent Best Practices
content: ✅ DO: - Assign independent tasks per agent - Use worktrees for isolation - Set reasonable timeouts - Check progress periodically - Batch similar tasks  ❌ DON'T: - Multiple agents modify same file - Overly complex single-agent tasks - Ignore agent outputs - Create too many parallel agents (5-10 optimal)  ---

title: Boris 使用技巧
keywords: 构建一个任务管理应用。

### 并行会话示例

**窗口 1：前端会话**

| 项目约定和架构上下文（自动读取） |
|

| 会话级笔记和决策 |

新会话开始时：

title: 核心原则
content: 大多数 AI 开发工具通过约束模型来构建：定义僵化的工作流、控制模型能看到什么、精确指定工具使用顺序。这以牺牲灵活性和能力为代价创造了可预测性。  Claude Code 的架构颠倒了这一点。  正如 Boris Cherny 所描述：  > "产品就是模型。"  方法是尽可能直接地暴露模型，使用最小的工具集和最小的脚手架，并允许模型确定给定任务的最佳方法。

title: 设计决策背后的理念
content: flowchart LR A[传统 AI 工具] --> B[约束模型] B --> C[僵化工作流] C --> D[可预测但能力受限]  E[Claude Code] --> F[暴露模型] F --> G[最小脚手架] G --> H[灵活且能力最大化]  style E fill:e8f5e9 style H fill:c8e6c9 style D fill:ffcdd2  为什么这样设计？   核心洞察： 一个能力较弱的模型需要更多 token 完成同样任务。使用更便宜的模型实际上更便宜并不明显。通常，最强大的模型更便宜且消耗更少 token，因为它以更少的修正周期完成任务。  ---

title: 关键指标（2026 年初）
content: > "Spotify 的工程师自 12 月以来就没有手动编写过代码。"

title: Boris 的个人工作流
content: 并行会话策略：  > "我启动一个任务，然后另一个，然后另一个，然后去喝杯咖啡让它们运行。"  Boris 通常同时运行 5 个或更多并行会话： - 一个构建认证系统 - 一个构建数据可视化层 - 一个编写测试 - 一个处理文档 - 一个监控 PR  权限管理：  > "一旦计划看起来不错，我就让模型执行。之后我自动接受编辑。用 Opus 4.6，它几乎每次都一次性正确完成。"  ---

title: Boris 的模型选择原则
content: Boris Cherny 专门使用 Opus 4.6，启用最大努力，从不降低能力来节省 token。  他的推理很精确：  > "因为一个能力较弱的模型不那么智能，它需要更多的 token 来完成同样的任务。使用更便宜的模型实际上更便宜并不明显。通常，最强大的模型更便宜且消耗更少的 token，因为它以更少的修正周期完成任务更快。"

title: 模型能力 vs Token 成本关系
content: graph TD A[选择模型] --> B{选择什么？} B -->|弱模型| C[需要更多修正周期] C --> D[更多 token 消耗] D --> E[总成本更高]  B -->|强模型| F[更少修正周期] F --> G[更少 token 消耗] G --> H[总成本更低]  style E fill:ffcdd2 style H fill:c8e6c9

title: 模型选择指南
content: Boris 的建议：  > "不要试图过早优化。给工程师他们需要的所有 token。在某些东西被证明并正在扩展时，再优化。不是之前。"  ---

title: Plan Mode 使用频率
content: Boris 在大约 80% 的会话中使用计划模式。  机制本身出奇地简单：注入模型上下文的一句话：  > "请先不要编写任何代码。"

title: 为什么规划决定结果
content:
一个经过审查和批准的完善计划，在编写任何代码之前，产生三个效果： 1. Claude 将其推理投入到正确的问题上 2. 意图和方法之间的不一致在嵌入代码之前被捕获 3. 结果实现是连贯的，因为它遵循连贯的设计 时间投资回报： 三十分钟的结构化规划经常将十小时的构建减少到三小时。 flowchart LR A[30分钟规划] --> B[3小时构建] C[0分钟规划] --> D[10小时构建
+多次返工] style A fill:c8e6c9 style B fill:c8e6c9 style D fill:ffcdd2

title: 规划的纪律
content: > "实际阅读 Claude 产生的计划。不要反射性地批准它。计划是你可以以最小成本干预的点。"  审查计划时检查： - 是否与设计意图一致？ - 是否会影响不应该触及的文件？ - 数据流是否正确？ - 边缘情况是否被处理？  ---

title: 核心诊断问题
content: Claude Code 产生的糟糕结果几乎从来不能归因于模型能力不足。  正确的诊断问题是：  > 我的规格是否足以产生我想要的东西？  在大多数情况下，答案是：不是。

title: 规格充分 vs 规格不足
content: 规格不足： 构建一个任务管理应用。  规格充分： 为一个三人团队构建一个任务管理应用程序。要求：  1. 任务有标题、可选描述、截止日期和优先级（低、中、高） 2. 每个任务可以分配给三个硬编码用户之一：Alice、Bob 或 Carol 3. 任务可以标记为完成，记录完成时间戳 4. 任务列表可以按受托人或优先级过滤 5. 所有数据持久化在 localStorage 中——不需要后端 6. 界面：干净、浅色主题；没有外部 CSS 框架  技术：仅 HTML、CSS、纯 JavaScript。  第二个版本关闭了 Claude 否则会通过假设解决的所有重要决策点。

title: 显式优于隐式
content: > "即使看起来明显，也要说明你想让 Claude 做什么。"  必须显式声明的： - 检查文档 → 在编写代码之前 - 特定库版本 → 指定它们 - 不更改现有文件 → 明确说明 - 特定文件结构 → 描述它  隐式期望是经常不满足的期望。显式指令是一致遵循的指令。  ---

title: 增量开发的理由
content: 每个实现的功能都是可以独立验证的行为单元。如果功能 2 建立在功能 1 之上而没有验证功能 1，缺陷就会复合。  基础中的缺陷向上传播，嵌入到建立在其上的所有东西中。  每个验证过的功能也是一个稳定的平台，可以在其上自信地构建下一个功能。

title: 构建周期
content: flowchart TD A[详细指定功能] --> B[进入计划模式] B --> C[审查计划] C --> D{计划合理？} D -->|否| E[修改计划] E --> B D -->|是| F[批准并实现] F --> G[测试功能] G --> H{正确工作？} H -->|否| I[解决差异] I --> F H -->|是| J[确认并前进] J --> A  style J fill:c8e6c9 style I fill:fff9c4

title: 构建顺序原则
content: 项目进度示例：   ---

title: 多会话并行策略
content: 多个 Claude Code 会话，每个分配特定范围，复制小团队的并行能力。  Boris 的工作流：  > "我启动一个任务，然后另一个，然后另一个，然后去喝杯咖啡让它们运行。"

title: Git Worktree 配合
content: 使用 Git 工作树隔离并行开发：  从 main 分支的仓库根目录 git worktree add ../my-project-auth feature/authentication git worktree add ../my-project-notifications feature/notifications  每个工作树有自己的磁盘文件集，可以独立开发。  完成后合并：  cd my-project git merge feature/authentication git merge feature/notifications

title: 并行会话示例
content: 窗口 1：前端会话 我们正在为笔记应用程序构建前端。后端 REST API 已经在端口 3001 上运行。你在这个会话中的范围只是 client/ 目录。  不要触及 server/ 目录中的任何文件。  首先进入计划模式。在写任何东西之前向我展示你的实现计划。  窗口 2：测试会话 我们正在为笔记应用程序 REST API 编写集成测试。你在这个会话中的范围 是 server/tests/ 目录。  不要修改 server/tests/ 之外的任何文件。  ---

title: 50% 实践
content: 有经验的 Claude Code 用户中的工作惯例：  > 当上下文使用达到 40-50% 时，开始新会话。  上下文饱和的症状是 漂移：Claude 开始做出与它在会话早期建立的约束不一致的决策。

title: 跨会话连续性
content: 四个连续性文档：   新会话开始时：  新会话。按顺序读取以下文件： 1. CLAUDE.md 2. PRD.md 3. README.md（特别是实现状态部分） 4. progress.md  确认你对项目状态的理解并告诉我我们停在哪里。  ---

title: 发布前验证
content: 在公开发布之前，Claude Code 在 Anthropic 内部运行了 四到五个月，在任何外部发布前都仔细研究了其行为。

title: 机制可解释性
content: 最深层的安全研究是机制可解释性：理解模型内部在单个计算组件层面实际发生了什么。  > "我们可以识别一个与欺骗相关的神经元。我们开始能够监控它并理解它正在激活。"

title: 安全优先的架构
content: Claude Code 的设计从一开始就考虑安全： - 权限请求机制 - PreToolUse hooks 验证 - PostToolUse 安全扫描 - 完整的审计日志  ---

title: 主动任务生成
content: Claude Code 开始基于观察到的项目信号生成可操作建议。  > "Claude 读取 Slack 反馈线程，检查 GitHub 问题跟踪器，审查遥测数据，并提出带有相关 PR 的建议：'这里有一些我可以做的事情。我已经提了几个 PR。想看看吗？'"

title: 超越软件开发
content: Boris 对当前状态的评估：  > "编码基本上已解决——至少是我做的那种编码。"  前沿正在扩展到相邻领域。

title: 为六个月后的模型构建
url: content/17-boris-tips.html#为六个月后的模型构建
content: Boris 最可操作的战略指导：  > "为六个月后的模型构建，而不是为今天的模型。"  含义：   ---

title: 实践 1：规划模式体验
content: 任务： 使用 Boris 的规划纪律完成一个功能。  1. 启动 Claude Code claude  2. 激活计划模式 终端：按 Shift+Tab 两次 VS Code：点击计划模式按钮  3. 描述任务 构建一个简单的待办事项应用。要求： - 添加、删除、标记完成任务 - 数据存储在 localStorage - 单页面 HTML/CSS/JS  请先不要编写任何代码。展示实现计划。  4. 阅读并审查计划 不要反射性批准！  5. 检查： - 是否有不必要的文件？ - 数据流是否正确？ - 是否处理了边缘情况？  6. 批准后才实现

title: 实践 2：并行会话
content: 任务： 运行两个并行 Claude Code 会话。  窗口 1：VS Code 打开项目 启动 Claude Code 扩展 范围：client/ 目录  窗口 2：终端 claude 范围：server/tests/ 目录  使用 Git worktree 避免冲突 git worktree add ../project-client feature/frontend git worktree add ../project-tests feature/tests

title: 实践 3：规格充分性测试
content: 任务： 对比规格不足和规格充分的输出差异。  测试 A：规格不足 claude > 构建一个登录页面  测试 B：规格充分 claude > 构建一个登录页面。要求： > - 用户名和密码输入框 > - 登录按钮和"忘记密码"链接 > - 错误提示区域 > - 深色主题，响应式设计 > - 仅使用 HTML/CSS，无框架 > - 表单验证：用户名至少3字符，密码至少8字符  对比两次输出的差异  ---

title: Claude Code 使用检查清单
content: - [ ] 是否使用计划模式（80% 会话） - [ ] 是否阅读并审查计划 - [ ] 是否使用显式、充分的规格 - [ ] 是否逐功能构建并验证 - [ ] 是否考虑并行会话 - [ ] 是否监控上下文使用（<50%） - [ ] 是否维护连续性文档 - [ ] 是否选择合适的模型（Opus for 复杂任务） - [ ] 是否为未来模型设计工作流  ---  学完本模块后，你将理解： - Claude Code "产品就是模型" 的设计哲学 - Anthropic 内部如何达到 10-30 PR/天/工程师 - Boris Cherny 的模型选择、规划、并行工作流策略 - 如何为六个月后的模型构建而非今天的
